aiashley — global digest Generated 09:56 UTC

Monday, May 25, 2026

International News · 16 Sources · 4 Regions

Daily Brief

Hopes for an end to the US-Israel war with Iran dominated markets and diplomacy today, with oil prices falling sharply as Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated a deal could be announced imminently [BBC, Financial Times]. However, Iran's foreign ministry cautioned that an agreement 'is not imminent,' and President Trump urged negotiators not to rush [BBC, Le Monde]. The potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, closed since February, would significantly ease global energy pressures. Meanwhile, Russia launched one of its heaviest attacks on Ukraine since the invasion began, firing hundreds of drones and the nuclear-capable Oreshnik hypersonic missile at Kyiv, killing four and injuring approximately 100 [BBC, RFI]. In Turkey, riot police stormed the headquarters of the main opposition CHP party following a court ruling that ousted its leadership, deepening concerns about democratic erosion under President Erdoğan [BBC, France 24]. The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo continues to escalate rapidly, with suspected cases now exceeding 900 and the WHO warning of severe impediments to containment due to ongoing conflict in Ituri province [Guardian, RFI, RNZ]. Asian markets rallied on Iran deal optimism, with Japan's Topix reaching an all-time high [Nikkei Asia].

Top Stories
01 Geopolitics

US-Iran Peace Deal Talks Intensify as Oil Prices Slide, but Tehran Cautions Agreement Not Imminent

  • US Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated from New Delhi that negotiators have a 'pretty solid thing on the table' and an agreement could be reached Monday, while President Trump said he instructed negotiators 'not to rush into' a deal
  • Iran's foreign ministry confirmed progress on 'a large portion of the issues' but stated no one can claim an agreement is imminent; reports indicate Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's location is undisclosed, complicating communications
  • The proposed memorandum of understanding reportedly includes a 60-day ceasefire extension, reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and further negotiations on Iran's nuclear program
  • Brent crude fell 5.5% to $97.90/barrel and US crude dropped 5.9% to $90.93 on Monday morning; Asian stock markets rose on the news
Coverage
BBC WorldDetailed coverage of both US and Iranian positions, noted intelligence reports about Khamenei's location France 24Focused on oil price movements and market reaction Financial TimesEmphasized Rubio's warning that US would find 'another way' if talks fail Le MondeHighlighted Trump tempering expectations The HinduEmphasized Iran's position that negotiations concern ending war, not nuclear issues Nikkei AsiaFocused on market impacts including Japan stocks hitting all-time high
02 Conflict

Russia Launches Massive Missile and Drone Barrage on Kyiv, Deploys Nuclear-Capable Oreshnik

  • Russia fired hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles at Ukraine Saturday night, with Kyiv as the main target; four killed and approximately 100 injured
  • Russia's defense ministry confirmed use of the Oreshnik hypersonic missile, capable of carrying nuclear warheads, marking only the third use of this weapon system in the war
  • Damage reported at approximately 50 locations including residential buildings, schools, an opera house, and a museum across Kyiv
  • EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas called the Oreshnik use 'reckless nuclear brinkmanship' and announced EU diplomats would meet to discuss increasing pressure on Russia
Coverage
BBC WorldBalanced coverage including Russia's claim the strikes responded to Ukrainian attacks on civilian infrastructure BBC EuropeAdditional coverage of Luhansk dormitory strike that Russia claims killed 21, which Ukraine denies targeting RFI EnglishEmphasized European condemnation and nuclear implications of Oreshnik use
03 Society

Turkish Riot Police Storm Opposition Party Headquarters After Court Ousts Leadership

  • Turkish riot police used tear gas to force entry into Republican People's Party (CHP) headquarters in Ankara on Sunday after party members barricaded entrances
  • An appeals court ruling Thursday declared party leader Özgür Özel's election null and void, ordering replacement by Kemal Kilicdaroğlu, who lost to Erdoğan in 2023 presidential election
  • Ankara's governor ordered police to 'implement the court decision' after Kilicdaroğlu's representatives requested 'necessary procedures' to take control of headquarters
  • Özel declared 'We are under attack' via social media as security forces entered; clashes reported between supporters of both leaders
Coverage
BBC WorldDescribed move as 'further cementing President Erdoğan's grip on power' BBC EuropeSame coverage as BBC World France 24Brief video coverage characterizing situation as 'political crisis'
04 Climate

DRC Ebola Outbreak Surpasses 900 Suspected Cases as Violence Impedes Response

  • WHO Director-General confirmed more than 900 suspected Ebola cases including 101 confirmed cases; DRC health ministry reported 204 deaths
  • Outbreak centered in Ituri province where nearly 5 million people live amid ongoing conflict; 1 in 4 need humanitarian assistance and 1 in 5 are internally displaced
  • Arson attacks destroyed two Ebola treatment centers last week, reflecting community distrust and anger in conflict-affected region
  • The outbreak involves the rarer Bundibugyo strain for which no approved vaccines or treatments exist; contact tracing and isolation are only proven containment methods
Coverage
The GuardianEmphasized attacks on health workers and impact of aid cuts on vulnerable communities RFI EnglishFocused on WHO statements and surveillance scaling efforts RNZHighlighted community fear, distrust, and the burning of health centers Al JazeeraQuestioned how the rare strain 'outran doctors'
05 Geopolitics

NATO Allies Seek Clarity as US Announces Contradictory Troop Deployment Decisions

  • President Trump announced 5,000 additional troops for Poland one week after cancelling a planned 4,000-troop deployment to the same country
  • Earlier this month, the US announced troops would be pulled out of Germany; some US forces are currently deployed to the Middle East for the Iran conflict
  • Secretary of State Rubio told NATO foreign ministers in Sweden that the US constantly reevaluates troop presence based on global commitments
  • Trump said the Poland decision was based on his relationship with Polish President Karol Nawrocki, whom he backed in last year's election
Coverage
BBC EuropeNoted the announcements 'have caused confusion among allies' of the trans-Atlantic defense organization
Also Today
  • Israel military issues evacuation warnings to 10 Lebanon villages citing Hezbollah ceasefire violations The Hindu
  • Toyota to cut overseas production by 83,000 vehicles through November due to prolonged Strait of Hormuz blockade Nikkei Asia
  • Trump administration pauses deportations to DRC over Ebola but strands woman in Kinshasa despite judge's return order The Guardian
  • Pacific Ocean temperatures rising fast, raising 80% chance of El Niño by July with potential for rare 'super' event RFI English
  • SpaceX prepares for largest IPO in history as Musk's rocket company plans Nasdaq listing Al Jazeera
  • Messi substituted with apparent hamstring issue three weeks before Argentina's World Cup title defense begins Al Jazeera
  • Chemical tank heating up in California evacuation zone as 40,000 residents remain displaced RNZ
  • Senegalese president fires longtime ally Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko, triggering political instability Le Monde
  • Myanmar military launches renewed offensives to reclaim rare earth mining regions and border trade routes The Hindu
  • Around 20 injured after man sprays unknown substance at Tokyo luxury mall ATM The Hindu
